title: polypodium involutum
intro: Polypodium involutum, also known as the creeping polypody, is a type of fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, offering numerous health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. The herbal preparations of Polypodium involutum typically involve the leaves, which can be dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for various forms of treatment and consumption.
